rubric.evidence: Real headless full-lifecycle run (persistent profile, WASM q8): first visit showed loader state=download-required with a 'Download model (~130 MB)' button; after downloading, the model auto-transitioned to ready and a real multi-turn chat produced genuine output — 'Does money buy happiness?' → 'Money can't buy you happiness' (screenshot _runtest/shots/dialogpt-conversational-desktop-light.png). Readout: backend WASM, context tokens 6, reply tokens 7, 0.40 s, 17.4 tok/s. 0 console errors. Runnability was pre-verified separately: pipeline()/generate() throws in _prepare_inputs_for_generation for this single-file ONNX export, so the demo runs a manual AutoModelForCausalLM forward-pass decode loop (like the GPT-2 demo) which returns real tokens.
rubric.notes: Model: onnx-community/DialoGPT-small-ONNX (ONNX of microsoft/DialoGPT-small); Xenova/DialoGPT-small|medium do not exist on HF, so the canonical onnx-community transformers.js build was used.

rubric.evidence: conformance io-shape-semantic=manual. A real run streams coherent continuation tokens conditioned on the EOS-joined dialogue: the reply 'Money can't buy you happiness' is a topically-consistent answer to the prompt, terminated when the model emitted its own <|endoftext|> token (endedOnEos). Multi-turn verified in the pre-check: a 3-turn history ('Hi…'/'I am fine…'/'What is your favourite food?') generated a valid continuation.
rubric.notes: DialoGPT replies are often short/terse — that's the model, framed honestly on the page ('can be short, repetitive, off-topic, or blunt — that's the model being itself, not a bug').

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id (onnx-community/DialoGPT-small-ONNX, linked to card), declares-task (text-generation), declares-backend (WASM), declares-quantisation (q8), inference-off-main-thread (Web Worker: dialogpt.js DialoGPTEngine → worker.js) all pass. At-a-glance also documents the 147M-Reddit lineage, 124M GPT-2 architecture, and the EOS turn mechanism.
rubric.notes: Loaded via AutoModelForCausalLM for a custom decode loop, stated on the page + in the API section with the reason (KV-cache path unsupported for this export).

rubric.evidence: conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass; auto-init-on-cached=manual, VERIFIED: after the first-visit download, a same-profile reload auto-initialised to state=ready with NO Download button (cachedHasDownloadBtn=false) and 0 console errors; the dark-media cached page also showed '283 MB cached' + ready with no button (_runtest/shots/dialogpt-conversational-desktop-dark.png). First visit correctly showed download-required + a Download button.
rubric.notes: Uses shared createModelLoader; no hand-rolled Load button. Per-model 'Clear cached model' control present.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region, errors-on-the-page, runs-without-webgpu-fallback all pass + honest-progress-retry-offline=manual. Download progress renders in the shared loader progress bar; per-turn status uses role=status aria-live=polite ('DialoGPT is replying…' → 'Reply ready.'); generation failures surface as an .err status and the bot bubble shows '(failed)'; createModelLoader Retry covers load recovery. WASM model needs no WebGPU.
rubric.notes: A 'thinking…' placeholder bubble is shown while the first token is pending.

rubric.evidence: conformance: controls-operable=manual. Verified controls: message text input (Enter-to-send + Send button), Reset chat, conversation-starter chips, a Greedy/Sample segmented group (role=group + aria-pressed) that shows/hides temp+top-k, and max-reply/temp/top-k range sliders with live <output> value labels. Driving Send produced the real reply above; the sampling toggle hides temp/top-k in greedy mode.
rubric.notes: All sliders have visible <label> + live output; buttons are ≥~40px; chips ≥2rem tall.

rubric.evidence: conformance: no-fake-output=manual. The only text shown in a bot bubble is streamed from the worker's decode loop; on failure it shows '(failed)', never a canned reply. The see-inside context view is built from the same turns array the worker joins with EOS, and the token-distribution bars are the model's real per-step probabilities.

conformance: images-have-alt=pass (no ; icon is an inline SVG data URI), buttons-have-names=pass. The chat log is an aria-live=polite region with aria-label; the sampling toggle uses role=group + aria-pressed; loader/turn status use role=status aria-live; the <|endoftext|> separators carry a descriptive title.
rubric.notes: Applied accessibility guidance: accessible names on all controls, aria-pressed on the segmented sampling control, live regions for async state.

rubric.evidence: conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass; parity-both-classes=manual. In the full-lifecycle run mobileOverflow=false at 360×740 DPR3; a forced light-theme mobile screenshot (_runtest/shots/dialogpt-conversational-mobile-lightforced.png) shows the header/eyebrow/lede wrapping cleanly with no horizontal overflow. Chat bubbles use max-inline-size + overflow-wrap:anywhere (92% on mobile); the controls row is flex-wrap; the ctx-view and token rows use overflow-wrap:anywhere / min-inline-size:0.
rubric.notes: Light (warm paper) and dark both legible and read (desktop-light/dark + mobile-lightforced screenshots).

rubric.evidence: conformance: inp-stays-low=manual. All inference (the forward-pass decode loop, softmax, top-k) runs in a Web Worker (DialoGPTEngine → worker.js); tokens stream to the chat bubble incrementally, so the input stays responsive during generation. Per-token DOM work is O(1) plus a bounded top-6 distribution render.
rubric.notes: Applied identify-inp-causes / break-up-long-tasks: off-main-thread inference + streamed incremental output.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive chat message list and control panel without horizontal overflow on mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex-wrap for 1D control/action rows; set min-inline-size:0 (and overflow-wrap:anywhere) on flex/grid items holding long unbreakable content; use logical properties and intrinsic sizing.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the chat-input-row, chat-actions, controls, and sample-row are flex-wrap rows; message bubbles and the EOS context view use overflow-wrap:anywhere + max-inline-size; token rows use min-inline-size:0; a 560px media query narrows the token grid and widens bubbles to 92%.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible chat message list with streaming assistant tokens in aria-live region
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Expose accessible names on all controls; use aria-pressed for toggle/segmented buttons; announce async state and streamed content in role=status / aria-live regions.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the chat log is aria-live=polite with an aria-label; the Greedy/Sample control is a role=group of aria-pressed buttons; loader + per-turn status use role=status aria-live=polite; every slider has a bound <label> + <output>.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance buttons-have-names=pass; live-status-region=pass; aria-pressed toggling verified in the build

guidanceConsulted.query: stream generated tokens without INP regressions
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy JS off the main thread and keep interaction handlers short to protect INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — model download + the forward-pass decode loop run in a Web Worker; decoded tokens stream incrementally to the bubble; the Send/keydown handlers do only trivial DOM work.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; worker.js runs model(makeInputs(ids)) per step and posts 'token' messages

guidanceConsulted.query: avoid blocking the main thread during generation
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up / bound long main-thread loops so they don't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the generation loop is entirely off-thread; there is no long synchronous main-thread loop; per-token main-thread work is O(1) + a bounded top-6 render.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: dialogpt.js renderDist bounded to 6 items; streaming callbacks update the DOM in small increments

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue-to-page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inherited from public/styles.css @view-transition navigation:auto (invariant 6); use-case cards use the shared .model-card grid.

openQuestions: DialoGPT context has no truncation guard — for a very long conversation the EOS-joined sequence could exceed the 1024-token window. Should the demo cap/trim history and surface that, as a NEW targeted fix?
Would a side-by-side 'DialoGPT vs a modern instruct chat model (e.g. SmolLM2)' comparison make the 2019-vs-now alignment gap concrete as a NEW additive demo?
Is q8 the right honesty/quality trade-off, or would fp32 noticeably improve reply coherence at ~2x the download?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for dialogpt-conversational done (desktop real-reply run + mobile/desktop light+dark screenshots read; cached auto-init verified); support flipped to ok. Re-audit if the design system changes.

Add a context-length guard that trims the oldest turns when the EOS-joined sequence approaches the 1024-token window, with a visible 'trimmed N earlier turns' note (targeted in-place fix, preserves identity).
Add a NEW additive demo comparing DialoGPT against a modern on-device instruct/chat model on the same prompts, to make the alignment/quality gap concrete (new slug, never replacing this page).

summary: Revision-1 build critique for dialogpt-conversational (text-generation/conversational, WASM q8, onnx-community/DialoGPT-small-ONNX), evidenced by a real headless full-lifecycle run: first-visit download → REAL multi-turn reply 'Money can't buy you happiness' (6 context tokens → 7 reply tokens, 17.4 tok/s); cached reload auto-inits with no Download button and 0 console errors. The star is the EOS-joined dialogue mechanism, shown live with the <|endoftext|> separators highlighted and the real next-token distribution. Distinct from the built instruct/chat LLMs (GPT-2, SmolLM2, TinyLlama, WebLLM chats): DialoGPT is an older GPT-2-based conversational model using EOS-joined history, framed honestly as a legible teaching example. Conformance 14/14 auto-checkable pass + 9 manual-evidenced; responsive desktop+mobile clean, light+dark legible.
